title: Rootful Podman for Windows fails to be rootful
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Podman install on windows does not run dnf install step in a Dockerfile successfully, even when run as root.

**Describe the results you expected:**

dnf install to work as part of a Dockerfile when building a new container image. I ran into a similar problem just running wsl ubi8-buildah where I couldn't run rootless podman because missing cgroups2 and rootful podman had nftables incompatibilities.

This is apparently a popular and easy setup in the rest of the Linux development community. I'd much rather use RedHat adjacent distributions to match what I use elsewhere, but this is really hard!

```
PS C:\Users\A> wsl -l -v
Windows Subsystem for Linux has no installed distributions.
Distributions can be installed by visiting the Microsoft Store:
https://aka.ms/wslstore
PS C:\Users\A> wsl --set-default-version 2
For information on key differences with WSL 2 please visit https://aka.ms/wsl2
The operation completed successfully.
PS C:\Users\A> podman machine init
Extracting compressed file
Importing operating system into WSL (this may take a few minutes on a new WSL install)...
Configuring system...
Generating public/private ed25519 key pair.
Your identification has been saved in podman-machine-default
Your public key has been saved in podman-machine-default.pub
<SHA256 data>
Machine init complete
To start your machine run:

        podman machine start

PS C:\Users\A> podman machine set --rootful
PS C:\Users\A> podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
API forwarding listening on: npipe:////./pipe/docker_engine

Docker API clients default to this address. You do not need to set DOCKER_HOST.
Machine "podman-machine-default" started successfully
PS C:\Users\A> cd .\podman\
PS C:\Users\A\podman> wsl -d podman-machine-default

You will be automatically entered into a nested process namespace where
systemd is running. If you need to access the parent namespace, hit ctrl-d
or type exit. This also means to log out you need to exit twice.

[user@example ~]$ cd /mnt/c/Users/A/podman/
[user@example podman]$ cat Dockerfile
FROM ubi8/ruby-27 AS build

RUN dnf install -y tzdata

CMD [sleep]
[user@example podman]$ sudo podman build -t example/test .
STEP 1/3: FROM ubi8/ruby-27 AS build
✔ registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ruby-27:latest
Trying to pull registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ruby-27:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Checking if image destination supports signatures
Copying blob 1b3417e31a5e done
Copying blob 809fe483e885 done
Copying blob 36bead343ed7 done
Copying blob 0ca677792a62 done
Copying blob cf6c0d5d0e6c done
Copying config f1c74b0b33 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2/3: RUN dnf install -y tzdata
Not root, Subscription Management repositories not updated

This system is not registered with an entitlement server. You can use subscription-manager to register.

Error: This command has to be run with superuser privileges (under the root user on most systems).
Error: error building at STEP "RUN dnf install -y tzdata": error while running runtime: exit status 1
[user@example podman]$ sudo su -
[root@example ~]# cd /mnt/c/Users/AGilmore/podman/
[root@example podman]# podman build -t example/test .
STEP 1/3: FROM ubi8/ruby-27 AS build
STEP 2/3: RUN dnf install -y tzdata
Not root, Subscription Management repositories not updated

This system is not registered with an entitlement server. You can use subscription-manager to register.

Error: This command has to be run with superuser privileges (under the root user on most systems).
Error: error building at STEP "RUN dnf install -y tzdata": error while running runtime: exit status 1
```


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
[user@example podman]$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.0
API Version:  4.2.0
Go Version:   go1.18.4
Built:        Thu Aug 11 08:42:17 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

Note, I installed the podman 4.2.1 msi downloaded from github

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
[root@example podman]# ls -l ../Downloads/podman-v4.2.1.msi
-rwxrwxrwx 1 user user 23846400 Sep 19 13:21 ../Downloads/podman-v4.2.1.msi

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
WSL running on Windows 10 21H2 Build 19044.1889

comments.body: @n1hility Have you seen anything like this?

Note that editing things like /etc/resolv.conf in the podman default
machine still requires root/sudo and it works.

On Tue, Sep 20, 2022, 06:53 Matt Heon ***@***.***> wrote:

> @n1hility <https://github.com/n1hility> Have you seen anything like this?
>
> —

Thanks! I figured it was something basic, but hadn't figured that. I appreciate it!
your welcome thanks for using podman!
